title: On-disk node store with zstd compression
purpose: Deliver DiskStore, the durable NodeStore implementation that persists content-addressed prolly tree nodes to the filesystem. Every node is written as a zstd-compressed file named by its hex hash; reads decompress on load and cache the decompressed node in memory. This is the component that turns the in-memory prolly tree (CORE-001) into a storage engine that survives process restarts — it is the persistence layer everything else in the core cluster depends on at runtime.
task: Implement DiskStore in crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs. DiskStore must satisfy the NodeStore trait defined in CORE-001. Each node is stored as a separate file in a caller-supplied directory, with the file name being the lowercase hex encoding of the node's BLAKE3 hash. Writes serialise the node (using the format from CORE-001), compress the bytes with zstd, and write the result atomically. Reads decompress the file contents and deserialise to a Node; the decompressed node is then inserted into an LRU cache so subsequent accesses are served from memory without re-reading or re-decompressing. DiskStore::new(path) requires the directory to already exist — it SHALL NOT create missing directories (P6). Wire DiskStore into store/mod.rs and re-export it from lib.rs. This brief covers only the disk store — WAL durable writes are CORE-005, shard actor wiring is CORE-007.

requirements.title: DiskStore struct and constructor
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a DiskStore struct that holds: a PathBuf pointing to the storage directory, an LRU cache mapping Hash to Node (decompressed), and a configurable cache capacity. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide DiskStore::new(dir: PathBuf, cache_capacity: usize) -> Result<DiskStore, StoreError> that opens an existing directory. IF the supplied path does not exist or is not a directory, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return Err(StoreError::DirectoryNotFound). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT create the directory when it is absent — the caller is responsible for provisioning the directory (P6). DiskStore SHALL implement Debug.
acceptance: DiskStore::new with an existing directory returns Ok(DiskStore)
DiskStore::new with a non-existent path returns Err(StoreError::DirectoryNotFound)
DiskStore::new with a path that is a file (not a directory) returns Err(StoreError::DirectoryNotFound)
No directory is created on disk by DiskStore::new under any error path
DiskStore implements Debug

files: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:1-1 — placeholder file for the complete `DiskStore` and `StoreError` implementation; currently only a comment.
crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:6-10 — current `NodeStore` trait signatures are infallible and will shape whether `DiskStore` can satisfy the brief literally.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:9-24 — `Hash` is copyable, orderable, and map-key-safe; use it as the LRU key.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:152-180 — `Node` is `Debug + Clone + PartialEq + Eq` with `serialise`, `deserialise`, and `hash`, so it can be cached decompressed.

docs/design/core/design.json:54-62 — core constraints: no files over 500 lines, no panics/unwraps in production, public types `Debug`, error types implement `Error`, and `mod.rs` stays declarations/re-exports only.
context: `disk.rs` exists but is effectively empty; treat this as implementing the module from scratch, not editing existing logic.
`DiskStore` should derive or implement `Debug`; all fields can be debugged if using `PathBuf` and `lru::LruCache<Hash, Node>` or an interior-mutable wrapper around it.
Constructor must use metadata checks only (`is_dir`/`try_exists`/`metadata`) and must not call `create_dir`, `create_dir_all`, or otherwise provision the directory.
Zero cache capacity is not mentioned in R1 but is required by R5 as `StoreError::InvalidCapacity`, so include that variant and check in `new` from the start.
scout.approach: Implement `pub struct DiskStore` in `disk.rs` with a private `dir: PathBuf`, an LRU cache keyed by `Hash` and storing decompressed `Node`, and enough state to enforce the requested capacity. `DiskStore::new(dir, cache_capacity)` should first reject `cache_capacity == 0`, then reject missing paths and file paths with `StoreError::DirectoryNotFound`, and only then build the cache; do not create or mutate the filesystem in the constructor. Keep helper methods such as `node_path(&Hash) -> PathBuf` private and compact so the new file remains below CN1’s 500-line limit.
scout.notes: There is an acceptance/API mismatch: the current `NodeStore::get(&self)` cannot mutate an ordinary LRU cache, so either the cache must use interior mutability, or `DiskStore` must expose fallible inherent methods with `&mut self`. Decide this before writing tests, because it affects every later requirement.

files_changed.note: Replaced placeholder with DiskStore fields, Debug derive, constructor validation, cache capacity tracking, and private path/cache helpers.
Added constructor tests covering existing directory, missing path, file path, no error-path directory creation, invalid zero capacity, and Debug formatting.

dev.how: DiskStore now holds the storage PathBuf, a RefCell-wrapped lru::LruCache<Hash, Node> containing decompressed nodes, and a NonZeroUsize capacity. DiskStore::new rejects zero capacity, uses fs::metadata/is_dir to open only pre-existing directories, maps missing/non-directory paths to StoreError::DirectoryNotFound, and never provisions directories.

checklist.note: DiskStore exists and participates in the complete NodeStore implementation delivered across R3/R4; the constructor establishes the directory and decompressed-node cache state.

acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:23-25 rejects metadata that is not a directory; test `constructor_accepts_only_existing_directories` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:48-51 writes a file and matches StoreError::DirectoryNotFound.

requirements.title: StoreError type
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a StoreError enum in crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs with at minimum the variants: DirectoryNotFound, Io(std::io::Error), Compression(String), Deserialise(String). THE SYSTEM SHALL implement std::error::Error and Display for StoreError. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use unwrap() or expect() in non-test code — all IO and compression failures propagate as StoreError.
acceptance: StoreError implements std::error::Error
StoreError implements Display with a human-readable message for each variant
StoreError::Io wraps std::io::Error
StoreError::Compression carries a String description of the zstd failure
cargo clippy -p haematite produces no warnings about unhandled Results in disk.rs

files: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:1-1 — define `StoreError` here alongside `DiskStore`; it currently has no types.
crates/haematite/src/error.rs:1-45 — existing project style for hand-written `Display`, `std::error::Error::source`, and `From<std::io::Error>`.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:35-60 — `NodeError` already implements `Debug`, `Display`, and `std::error::Error`; deserialisation errors can be stringified from here.
crates/haematite/Cargo.toml:15-29 — lint settings deny unsafe, clippy groups, unwrap/expect, and panic, so production error paths must be fully propagated.
context: R2 lists minimum variants only; R5 also requires `InvalidCapacity`, so add it now instead of retrofitting later.
`std::io::Error` is not `Clone` or `PartialEq`, so tests should use `matches!` rather than deriving equality for `StoreError`.

Compression/decompression functions in `zstd` return `std::io::Error`; map those to `StoreError::Compression(String)` when the failure is from zstd, and reserve `StoreError::Io` for filesystem operations.
Deserialisation failures come from `Node::deserialise`/`NodeError`; convert with `.to_string()` into `StoreError::Deserialise(String)`.
scout.approach: Define `#[derive(Debug)] pub enum StoreError { DirectoryNotFound, InvalidCapacity, Io(std::io::Error), Compression(String), Deserialise(String) }`. Implement `fmt::Display` with human-readable messages for every variant, implement `std::error::Error` with `source()` returning `Some` for the `Io` variant, and implement `From<std::io::Error> for StoreError` only where generic IO conversion is unambiguous. Avoid `.unwrap()`, `.expect()`, `panic!`, ignored `Result`s, and test-only shortcuts in non-test code.
scout.notes: Be careful not to use `?` on `zstd::encode_all`/`decode_all` if `From<std::io::Error>` maps everything to `Io`; the acceptance for corrupt compressed files expects `Compression` or `Deserialise`, not a generic IO error.

files_changed.note: Added StoreError enum, Display, Error::source, From<std::io::Error>, and explicit compression/deserialisation mapping.
Added tests for StoreError as std::error::Error and non-empty human-readable Display messages.

dev.how: StoreError includes DirectoryNotFound, InvalidCapacity, Io(std::io::Error), Compression(String), and Deserialise(String). Filesystem errors propagate as Io, zstd encode/decode failures map to Compression, and Node::deserialise errors map to Deserialise without unwrap/expect/panic in production code.

checklist.note: The error type supports fallible DiskStore NodeStore operations and preserves IO/compression/deserialisation distinctions required for compressed disk nodes.

acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:148-158 implements std::error::Error; test `store_error_traits_and_messages_are_available` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:61-65 type-checks StoreError as std::error::Error.

acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:136-146 implements Display arms for DirectoryNotFound, InvalidCapacity, Io, Compression, and Deserialise; test `store_error_traits_and_messages_are_available` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:66-75 checks non-empty messages for every variant.

acceptance.criterion: StoreError::Io wraps std::io::Error
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:131 defines Io(std::io::Error), :151 exposes it via Error::source, and :160-164 implements From<std::io::Error>.

acceptance.criterion: StoreError::Compression carries a String description of the zstd failure
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:132 defines Compression(String); zstd encode/decode failures are converted with error.to_string() at :177-183.

acceptance.criterion: cargo clippy -p haematite produces no warnings about unhandled Results in disk.rs
acceptance.evidence: `cargo clippy -p haematite -- -D warnings` exited 0; disk.rs explicitly handles write/sync/persist Results at crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:90-96.

requirements.title: NodeStore::put — serialise, compress, write
requirements.spec: WHEN NodeStore::put(node) is called on a DiskStore, THE SYSTEM SHALL serialise the node to bytes using the format defined in CORE-001, compress the serialised bytes with zstd, and write the compressed bytes to a file named <hex-hash> in the configured directory. THE SYSTEM SHALL return the BLAKE3 Hash of the node. IF a file with that name already exists, THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT overwrite it — the content-addressed scheme guarantees the file already contains the correct bytes. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT write partial files that are visible to concurrent readers: the write path is write-to-temp-then-rename on the same filesystem.
acceptance: After DiskStore::put(node), a file named by the node's hex hash exists in the directory
The file contents are zstd-compressed: zstd::decode_all on the file bytes succeeds
Decompressing and deserialising the file contents produces a node equal to the original
Calling put with the same node twice does not overwrite the first file (file mtime is unchanged)
put returns the same Hash as node.hash()

files: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:1-1 — implement the write path here, including hash-to-filename, compression, temp write, and rename.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:158-180 — use `Node::serialise()` and `Node::hash()`; do not invent a second node format.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:26-33 — `Hash` display is lowercase 64-character hex, exactly suitable for `<hex-hash>` filenames.
crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:6-10 — current trait `put(&mut self, &Node) -> Hash` cannot return `StoreError`, which conflicts with R3’s fallible IO/compression path.
docs/design/decisions.json:102-116 — ADR-006 says zstd compression applies per tree node; WAL stays uncompressed and is out of scope.
context: The file name should be `hash.to_string()`; `Hash::Display` already emits lowercase hex from the 32-byte content hash.
Existing files must not be overwritten. Check `target_path.exists()`/`try_exists()` before writing and return the hash immediately on hit; this preserves mtime acceptance.

Atomic visibility means write compressed bytes to a temp file in the same directory, flush/sync as appropriate, then `rename` to the final `<hex-hash>` path. On Unix, `rename` replaces an existing target, so avoid racing into replacement by checking again just before rename or by using a no-clobber persist strategy if available.

R3 says `NodeStore::put` but current `NodeStore` has no error channel. Tests generated from this brief may call an inherent `DiskStore::put(&Node) -> Result<Hash, StoreError>`; a literal trait call cannot satisfy IO error propagation.
scout.approach: Implement a private `write_node(&mut self, node: &Node) -> Result<Hash, StoreError>` or a public inherent `put(&mut self, node: &Node) -> Result<Hash, StoreError>` that computes `let hash = node.hash()`, builds the final path from `hash.to_string()`, returns early if the file already exists, serialises with `node.serialise()`, compresses with `zstd::encode_all`, writes to a uniquely named temp path under `dir`, and renames it to the final file on the same filesystem. After a successful write, optionally insert the decompressed `Node` clone into the LRU so a following get is a cache hit, but do not let cache insertion change the returned hash.
scout.notes: Temp-name generation must avoid panics and collisions without bringing in unrelated persistence machinery. A small helper using the hash plus process id and a retry counter is enough, or add a dev/regular dependency that safely persists temp files in the target directory; just keep all temp files in `dir` so rename is same-filesystem.

files_changed.note: Implemented put/write path: node hash naming, CORE-001 serialisation, zstd compression, same-directory temporary file persistence, no-clobber finalisation, and cache insertion.

Made NodeStore fallible with an associated error so DiskStore can implement NodeStore::put without swallowing StoreError; MemoryStore preserves its infallible inherent API and uses Infallible for the trait.
Added lru and tempfile dependencies used by DiskStore's cache and safe temporary-file persistence.
Added tests for compressed file creation, zstd decode/deserialise round-trip, hash return value, no overwrite/mtime preservation, and NodeStore trait put/get.

dev.how: DiskStore::put computes node.hash(), returns early when the content-addressed file already exists, serialises with Node::serialise, compresses with zstd, writes a NamedTempFile inside the storage directory, syncs it, and finalises with persist_noclobber so an existing target is never overwritten. The returned hash is always the node's BLAKE3 content hash.
dev.deviation: The scout called out the existing infallible NodeStore mismatch and suggested either inherent fallible methods or adapting around it. I changed NodeStore to a fallible associated-error trait so DiskStore can literally implement NodeStore::put with StoreError propagation; MemoryStore keeps compatible inherent get/put methods. I also used tempfile's same-directory no-clobber persistence instead of a custom hash/process-id temp-name retry helper.

checklist.note: DiskStore implements NodeStore::put and writes zstd-compressed content-addressed node files in the configured directory.

acceptance.criterion: After DiskStore::put(node), a file named by the node's hex hash exists in the directory
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:65-75 computes node.hash(), derives node_path, and writes compressed bytes; test `put_writes_zstd_compressed_node_under_content_hash_without_overwrite` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:80-88 asserts the hash-named path is a file.

acceptance.criterion: The file contents are zstd-compressed: zstd::decode_all on the file bytes succeeds
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:73-75 serialises then compresses before writing; test `put_writes_zstd_compressed_node_under_content_hash_without_overwrite` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:90-91 reads the file and successfully runs zstd::stream::decode_all.

acceptance.criterion: Decompressing and deserialising the file contents produces a node equal to the original
acceptance.evidence: test `put_writes_zstd_compressed_node_under_content_hash_without_overwrite` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:90-93 decodes zstd bytes, deserialises with Node::deserialise, and asserts equality with the original node.

acceptance.criterion: Calling put with the same node twice does not overwrite the first file (file mtime is unchanged)
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:68-70 returns early when the hash path exists and :93-96 uses persist_noclobber for races; test `put_writes_zstd_compressed_node_under_content_hash_without_overwrite` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:95-101 asserts unchanged mtime after a second put.

acceptance.criterion: put returns the same Hash as node.hash()
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:66 computes hash = node.hash() and :77 returns it; test `put_writes_zstd_compressed_node_under_content_hash_without_overwrite` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:84-86 asserts equality.

requirements.title: NodeStore::get — read, decompress, cache
requirements.spec: WHEN NodeStore::get(hash) is called on a DiskStore and the hash is in the LRU cache, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the cached Node without touching the filesystem. WHEN the hash is not in the cache and a file named by its hex encoding exists in the directory, THE SYSTEM SHALL read the file, decompress the bytes with zstd, deserialise to a Node, insert the decompressed Node into the LRU cache, and return Some(Node). IF no file for that hash exists in the directory, THE SYSTEM SHALL return None. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT store compressed bytes in the LRU cache — cached nodes are stored decompressed in memory (ADR-006).
acceptance: DiskStore::get(hash) returns None for a hash that was never put
DiskStore::get(hash) after a put returns Some(node) equal to the put node

A second DiskStore::get for the same hash is served from the LRU cache (verified by putting the node with one DiskStore instance, evicting the cache, then getting with a fresh instance that has an empty cache — file read succeeds)
The LRU cache holds decompressed Nodes: a cache hit does not call zstd::decode_all
DiskStore::get with a hash whose file exists but is corrupted returns Err(StoreError::Compression) or Err(StoreError::Deserialise)

files: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:1-1 — implement read, decompress, deserialize, and cache-hit behavior here.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:166-173 — `Node::deserialise` returns `NodeError` for invalid tags/truncated/trailing/unsorted decoded nodes; map this to `StoreError::Deserialise`.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:35-60 — `NodeError` display messages are already human-readable for `Deserialise(String)`.
crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:6-10 — current `NodeStore::get(&self) -> Option<Node>` cannot propagate compression/deserialisation errors or update an ordinary LRU cache.
docs/design/decisions.json:111-115 — ADR-006 consequence: reads decompress on load and cached nodes are decompressed in memory.
context: The cache value must be `Node`, not compressed bytes, to satisfy ADR-006 and the acceptance criterion that cache hits skip `zstd::decode_all`.

An ordinary `LruCache` updates recency on `get`, which usually requires mutable access. If conforming to `NodeStore::get(&self)`, use `RefCell<LruCache<...>>`; if using an inherent fallible method, prefer `&mut self` for simpler borrowing.
Missing files are not errors for `get`: map `ErrorKind::NotFound` to `Ok(None)` before generic IO conversion.

Corrupt file cases need two different mappings: unreadable filesystem errors to `Io`, invalid compressed bytes to `Compression(String)`, and valid compressed but invalid node bytes to `Deserialise(String)`.
scout.approach: Build a fallible read helper around `node_path(hash)`: first look in the LRU and return a cloned cached node if present, then read the compressed file bytes, treating `NotFound` as `None`. Decompress with `zstd::decode_all` into serialized node bytes, call `Node::deserialise`, insert the decompressed `Node` into the LRU under the hash, and return `Some(node)`. Add tests that remove or corrupt the backing file after the first read to prove cache hits do not touch/decompress the filesystem, plus tests with a fresh `DiskStore` to prove cold reads work from disk.
scout.notes: This is the requirement most exposed by the trait mismatch: the brief explicitly expects `Err(StoreError::Compression)` or `Err(StoreError::Deserialise)`, which the current `NodeStore::get` signature cannot express. Do not silently return `None` for corrupt data in the primary API; that would break crash-recovery assumptions.

files_changed.note: Implemented get/read path with cache hit fast path, disk read, zstd decompression, Node::deserialise, cache insertion, and corrupt-data error mapping.
NodeStore was made fallible so DiskStore::get can return StoreError::Compression or StoreError::Deserialise instead of losing corruption information.
Added tests for missing hashes, cold reads from a fresh store, cache hits after backing file removal, corrupted compressed files, and valid zstd/invalid-node deserialisation errors.

dev.how: DiskStore::get first checks the decompressed LRU cache and returns a cloned Node without filesystem access on hit. On miss it reads <hex-hash>, treats NotFound as Ok(None), decodes zstd bytes, deserialises via Node::deserialise, inserts the decompressed Node into the LRU, and distinguishes IO, Compression, and Deserialise failures.
dev.deviation: As in R3, I resolved the trait mismatch by making NodeStore fallible with an associated error so trait-based DiskStore::get can return StoreError rather than silently returning None for corrupt data. Interior mutability is still used for cache recency updates because get takes &self.

checklist.note: DiskStore implements NodeStore::get for zstd-compressed content-addressed node files.
Reads decompress zstd on load and the LRU stores decompressed Node values, not compressed byte buffers.

stories.note: The node-store portion of crash recovery is covered: after reopening with a fresh DiskStore, existing compressed node files are read, decompressed, deserialised, and cached; WAL recovery remains outside CORE-004 by boundary.

acceptance.criterion: DiskStore::get(hash) returns None for a hash that was never put
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:52-56 returns Ok(None) on ErrorKind::NotFound; test `get_reads_missing_disk_and_cached_nodes_correctly` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:107-110 asserts None for an unknown hash.

acceptance.criterion: DiskStore::get(hash) after a put returns Some(node) equal to the put node
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:58-62 decompresses/deserialises/caches and returns Some(node); test `get_reads_missing_disk_and_cached_nodes_correctly` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:112-116 puts then reads with a fresh DiskStore and asserts Some(node).

acceptance.criterion: A second DiskStore::get for the same hash is served from the LRU cache (verified by putting the node with one DiskStore instance, evicting the cache, then getting with a fresh instance that has an empty cache — file read succeeds)
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:47-50 checks cache first and returns without fs::read; test `get_reads_missing_disk_and_cached_nodes_correctly` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:115-119 first populates a fresh reader from disk, deletes the backing file, then a second get still returns Some(node), proving the LRU cache served the hit.

acceptance.criterion: The LRU cache holds decompressed Nodes: a cache hit does not call zstd::decode_all
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:16 stores LruCache<Hash, Node>, :58-62 inserts only after zstd decode and Node::deserialise, and :104-106 returns a cloned Node on cache hit. The file-removal cache-hit test at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:118-119 would fail if the hit touched disk/decode.

acceptance.criterion: DiskStore::get with a hash whose file exists but is corrupted returns Err(StoreError::Compression) or Err(StoreError::Deserialise)
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:58-60 maps zstd decode failures to Compression and Node::deserialise failures to Deserialise; test `corrupt_files_return_compression_or_deserialisation_errors` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:153-173 covers both corrupt zstd bytes and valid-zstd invalid-node bytes.

requirements.title: LRU cache eviction and capacity
requirements.spec: WHILE DiskStore is in operation, THE SYSTEM SHALL maintain an LRU cache of at most cache_capacity decompressed Nodes. WHEN the cache is at capacity and a new Node is inserted, THE SYSTEM SHALL evict the least recently used entry. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT grow the cache beyond the configured capacity — unbounded growth is prohibited. THE SYSTEM SHALL use the lru crate (or equivalent) rather than a hand-rolled structure.
acceptance: A DiskStore with cache_capacity=1 holds at most one decompressed Node at a time
Inserting a second node into a capacity-1 cache evicts the first
After eviction, getting the evicted hash causes a disk read (not a cache hit)
DiskStore::new(dir, 0) returns Err(StoreError::InvalidCapacity) — a zero-capacity cache is an error, not silently unbounded (P6).

files: crates/haematite/Cargo.toml:10-13 — dependencies currently include `blake3`, `zstd`, and `beamr`; `lru` is not yet declared.
crates/haematite/Cargo.toml:15-29 — strict lints mean dependency use and tests must be clippy-clean without unwrap/expect/panic.
crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:1-1 — cache type, capacity validation, and eviction behavior all belong in this new implementation file.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:9-24 — `Hash` derives the traits needed for an LRU key.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:152-156 — `Node` is cloneable/equatable, which makes decompressed cache storage and eviction tests straightforward.
context: Add an `lru` crate dependency (or a clearly equivalent crate) rather than hand-rolling eviction; the brief explicitly requires this.
Modern `lru` APIs commonly require a non-zero capacity type; this aligns with the acceptance that `cache_capacity = 0` returns `StoreError::InvalidCapacity`.

Because fields should stay private, eviction tests may need test-only helper methods under `#[cfg(test)]` such as `cache_len()`/`contains_cached()` or observable behavior via file deletion/corruption after eviction.
If `put` inserts into the cache, then a capacity-1 store putting node A then node B should evict A immediately. If `put` does not insert, reads must still enforce the same bound on insertion.
scout.approach: Add the LRU dependency in `Cargo.toml`, construct it only after `cache_capacity` has been validated non-zero, and centralize insertion through a helper like `cache_put(hash, node)` so both put-read and cold-read paths share eviction behavior. Use the cache’s own `len`, `get`, `put`, and `contains` APIs rather than maintaining a separate manual list. Test capacity 1 with two distinct nodes: insert/read the first, insert/read the second, then make the first file observably unavailable or corrupted and verify a later get is a disk read rather than a cache hit.
scout.notes: If using `RefCell` to satisfy `NodeStore::get(&self)`, clippy can complain about borrow patterns if a borrow is held across IO/decompression. Keep cache lookups/inserts in small scopes so the mutable borrow is dropped before filesystem work.

files_changed.note: Added lru crate dependency required by the requirement.
Constructed LruCache with NonZeroUsize capacity, rejected zero capacity, centralised cache get/put through lru APIs, and exposed cache_capacity for verification.
Added capacity-one eviction test proving the first node is evicted and later read attempts go back to disk.

dev.how: The DiskStore cache is an lru::LruCache<Hash, Node> whose capacity is created only from NonZeroUsize. Cache insertions use LruCache::put, cache hits use get to update recency, and DiskStore::new(dir, 0) returns StoreError::InvalidCapacity rather than creating an unbounded cache.

checklist.note: The bounded LRU contains decompressed Nodes and evicts through the lru crate rather than a hand-rolled structure.

acceptance.criterion: A DiskStore with cache_capacity=1 holds at most one decompressed Node at a time
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:21-31 constructs lru::LruCache with NonZeroUsize capacity and :109-111 inserts via LruCache::put; test `capacity_one_cache_evicts_the_least_recently_used_node` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:123-137 exercises capacity 1.

acceptance.criterion: Inserting a second node into a capacity-1 cache evicts the first
acceptance.evidence: test `capacity_one_cache_evicts_the_least_recently_used_node` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:126-133 puts first then second into capacity-1 cache, removes the first backing file, and get(first_hash) returns None rather than the cached first node.

acceptance.criterion: After eviction, getting the evicted hash causes a disk read (not a cache hit)
acceptance.evidence: test `capacity_one_cache_evicts_the_least_recently_used_node` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:132-133 removes the first node's disk file after eviction and get(first_hash) returns None, which proves it attempted the disk path instead of serving a cache hit.

acceptance.criterion: DiskStore::new(dir, 0) returns Err(StoreError::InvalidCapacity) — a zero-capacity cache is an error, not silently unbounded (P6).
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:21-22 converts cache_capacity with NonZeroUsize::new and returns StoreError::InvalidCapacity on zero; test `constructor_accepts_only_existing_directories` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:53-56 matches InvalidCapacity and asserts no directory creation.

requirements.title: Module wiring and public re-exports
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add DiskStore and StoreError to store/mod.rs via pub mod disk; and pub use disk::{DiskStore, StoreError}. THE SYSTEM SHALL re-export DiskStore and StoreError from the crate root lib.rs alongside the existing NodeStore and MemoryStore exports. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT add any logic to mod.rs beyond the declarations and re-exports (CN3).
acceptance: store/mod.rs contains pub mod disk and pub use disk::{DiskStore, StoreError} — no other additions
use haematite::{DiskStore, StoreError} compiles in an external crate test
cargo check -p haematite succeeds after wiring

files: crates/haematite/src/store/mod.rs:1-8 — already has `pub mod disk;` and `pub use memory::{MemoryStore, NodeStore};`; add only `pub use disk::{DiskStore, StoreError};`.
crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:1-17 — crate root currently re-exports `MemoryStore`/`NodeStore` but not `DiskStore`/`StoreError`.
crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:1-1 — public `DiskStore` and `StoreError` must exist before re-export wiring will compile.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mod.rs:1-8 — example of module-root convention: only `pub mod` declarations and `pub use` re-exports, no logic.
context: `store/mod.rs` already declares `pub mod disk`; R6 only needs the public re-export line once `disk.rs` defines the types.
`lib.rs` should re-export through `store::{DiskStore, MemoryStore, NodeStore, StoreError}` rather than reaching into `store::disk` directly.
CN3 is active: do not add tests, helper constants, functions, type aliases with behavior, or constructor logic to any `mod.rs`.

A simple external-use compile check can be approximated by a doc test or tiny integration test importing `use haematite::{DiskStore, StoreError};`, but there is no existing `tests/` directory in the crate.
scout.approach: After `disk.rs` compiles, update `store/mod.rs` to include `pub use disk::{DiskStore, StoreError};` while leaving all other lines declaration/re-export only. Then update `lib.rs` line 16 to re-export `DiskStore` and `StoreError` alongside `MemoryStore` and `NodeStore`. Keep module wiring as the final step so intermediate missing-type errors do not obscure implementation errors in `disk.rs`.
scout.notes: `store/mod.rs` already satisfies part of the acceptance (`pub mod disk;` exists), so avoid unnecessary reordering churn unless `cargo fmt` requires it.

files_changed.note: Added pub use disk::{DiskStore, StoreError}; while keeping mod.rs limited to pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports.
Re-exported DiskStore and StoreError from the crate root alongside MemoryStore and NodeStore.
Integration test imports haematite::{DiskStore, StoreError, NodeStore}, exercising external crate-root visibility.

dev.how: store/mod.rs already declared pub mod disk; it now re-exports DiskStore and StoreError. lib.rs re-exports the same types through store::{DiskStore, MemoryStore, NodeStore, StoreError}, so external code can use haematite::{DiskStore, StoreError}.

acceptance.criterion: store/mod.rs contains pub mod disk and pub use disk::{DiskStore, StoreError} — no other additions
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/mod.rs:1-9 contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports, including pub mod disk at :2 and pub use disk::{DiskStore, StoreError} at :8; `grep -n 'fn ' crates/haematite/src/store/mod.rs` produced no matches.

acceptance.criterion: use haematite::{DiskStore, StoreError} compiles in an external crate test
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:16 re-exports DiskStore and StoreError from the crate root; integration test crate imports `haematite::{DiskStore, ..., StoreError}` at crates/haematite/tests/disk_store.rs:6 and compiled/passed under `cargo test -p haematite`.

acceptance.criterion: cargo check -p haematite succeeds after wiring
acceptance.evidence: `cargo check -p haematite` exited 0 after the lib.rs/store/mod.rs wiring; root export is at crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:16 and store re-export is at crates/haematite/src/store/mod.rs:8-9.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement WAL durable writes or WAL file management — those are CORE-005
SHALL NOT implement shard actor wiring or route DiskStore into the shard supervision tree — that is CORE-007
SHALL NOT implement branching, merging, or snapshot operations
SHALL NOT create directories at runtime — the caller must provision the directory before constructing DiskStore (P6)
SHALL NOT implement tree traversal or mutation — those are CORE-002
SHALL NOT add a second node store implementation beyond DiskStore in this brief
verification: cargo check -p haematite succeeds with no errors
cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests including disk store tests
cargo clippy -p haematite -- -D warnings produces no errors
grep -rn 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs finds zero matches outside #[cfg(test)] blocks
grep -n 'fn ' crates/haematite/src/store/mod.rs finds no function definitions (mod.rs must be declarations only)
wc -l crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs shows fewer than 500 lines
ls <data-dir> after a put shows a file whose name matches the hex hash returned by put
file <data-dir>/<hex-hash> identifies the file as zstd-compressed data (or: zstd -d <file> succeeds)
